title: Problem updating the dependency column using API

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
From the API playground:

mutation{change_multiple_column_values(board_id:654, item_id:456,
  column_values: "{
  \"notes\" : \"y\",
  \"effort\":\"3\",
  \"dependent_on\":{\"item_ids\": [123,345]}
}"){id}}

returns error: SyntaxError: Unexpected token < in JSON at position 0
same syntax, changing “dependent_on” column to a “connect_boards” column with appropriate change in item id, works correctly

Any ideas?

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml: Can anyone confirm that the API to update a dependency column even works?

posts.author: dipro (Dipro Bhowmik)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Jim,

You should be able to update the Dependency column in a similar way to the item link column; so your syntax is correct.

In fact, I was able to reproduce this issue. I’ve gone ahead and reported this to our developers and will update here when this is fixed!

Dipro

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
@dipro

Thank you for the update.

posts.author: Trespaser5 (Kyle Tansill)
posts.cookedHtml: I am having exactly the same problem. Any help on a working syntax to get us by @dipro ? I am short on time

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Trespaser5

This is now working. The syntax I gave at the top is correct.

posts.author: Trespaser5 (Kyle Tansill)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks @JCorrell - this is fixed for me too

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml: This topic was automatically closed 7 days after the last reply. New replies are no longer allowed.
